task: Analyse a real-world dataset (e.g. Titanic) and produce a statistical report
reason: Statistical thinking is the backbone of every ML algorithm.

task: Analyse the Titanic dataset: compute summary statistics, visualise distributions, and run a χ² test
reason: Statistical foundations are essential before tackling any ML algorithm.

task: Build a real-world advanced-level project that demonstrates practical mastery of Machine Learning
reason: Hands-on practice at the advanced level advances your journey towards Machine Learning mastery.

task: Develop a Python script that simulates data from different underlying distributions (e.g., Bernoulli, Uniform, Exponential) and then repeatedly samples from these populations to demonstrate and visually explain the Central Limit Theorem, showing how the distribution of sample means approaches a normal distribution regardless of the original data's distribution.
reason: This hands-on simulation reinforces foundational statistical concepts of sampling distributions and the Central Limit Theorem, crucial for understanding inferential statistics, confidence intervals, and various model assumptions in ML, building on previous exposure to data analysis.

task: Containerize a pre-trained advanced Machine Learning model (e.g., a multi-label image classifier or a complex NLP model), develop a production-ready RESTful API using FastAPI or Flask to serve real-time predictions, and deploy it to a cloud platform like AWS Sagemaker Endpoints or Google Cloud Run, ensuring basic monitoring and auto-scaling capabilities.
reason: This task moves beyond basic model deployment by requiring containerization, robust API development, and cloud-native scaling/monitoring, essential skills for an AI/ML Engineer deploying production systems.

task: Design and implement an advanced RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) system capable of answering complex, multi-hop questions over a private document corpus. Integrate techniques like query re-writing, hybrid search (keyword + semantic), and a re-ranking mechanism (e.g., using a cross-encoder). Develop a custom evaluation benchmark with a mix of factual and reasoning-based questions, and quantitatively measure the system's precision, recall, and relevance, justifying the choice of LLM and embedding models.
reason: Building on intermediate LLM understanding, this advanced project focuses on critical architectural patterns and evaluation methodologies for robust, production-grade LLM applications.
